danger_signals.visual_form: Harry and Marv character sprites in hostile posture
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to Kevin; direct combat/evasion required

danger_signals.visual_form: Rats, bats, spiders, ghosts in basement environment
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger zone; hazard avoidance required after item collection phase

danger_signals.visual_form: Transition to basement level with supernatural enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Shift from preparation to survival mode; increased danger intensity

danger_signals.visual_form: Final confrontation in safe room basement area
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger point; must defeat to progress to safe room lockdown

safety_signals.visual_form: Fortified basement safe area where items are sent via chute
safety_signals.meaning: Goal location; represents family wealth protection and Kevin's victory condition

safety_signals.visual_form: Scattered household objects throughout mansion rooms
safety_signals.meaning: Resource gathering zones; safe to traverse during prep phase before bandits arrive

safety_signals.visual_form: Household objects deployed as weapons (traps trigger on contact)
safety_signals.meaning: Kevin's defense mechanism; safe when positioned away from traps

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple rooms and wings (toys, electronics, pets sections)
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation options; allows evasion routing and tactical positioning

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Can interact with household items; presence in multiple mansion rooms

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Burglar duo; criminal antagonists from film canon
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Two distinct character sprites (Harry and Marv); hostile animation states
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear in house after prep phase; signal threat activation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Rats, bats, spiders, ghosts; environmental hazards not aligned with bandits
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct creature sprites for each hazard type
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Confined to basement zone; appear only after item collection phase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mansion rooms fully accessible with scattered items; no enemy presence; normal household atmosphere
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kevin collects household objects to use as trap materials; free movement through all accessible areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Harry and Marv enter house; transition to evasion/combat visuals; sprites show hostile animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kevin must evade bandits while completing item collection; traps can be triggered to incapacitate enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to basement environment; supernatural enemies (rats, bats, spiders, ghosts) become visible; darker aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kevin navigates hazard gauntlet; boss encounter triggers; combat/survival focus

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fortified vault visualization; family riches secured; safe visual indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kevin secures McCallister wealth; game objective achieved; threats neutralized or bypassed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kevin McCallister screaming face sprite OR large 'Oh no!' text bubble on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Caught by bandits or failed to secure safe room; mission failure state

environmental_storytelling.example: House expanded into sprawling mansion with dedicated wings (toys, electronics, pets) signaling wealth and value to protect; haunted basement represents escalating danger as Kevin descends
Game explicitly set after Home Alone film events; Harry and Marv return from jail seeking revenge with backup, establishing narrative stakes beyond single-burglary scenario
Scattered household objects throughout rooms serve dual purpose: trap materials AND environmental markers of what family treasures exist to be protected via basement safe
Basement location houses rats, bats, spiders, ghosts—not encountered in upper mansion—creating sense of environmental danger increasing as Kevin approaches safe room

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Absent from gameplay; establish threat through narrative/UI presence
Patrolling house; can be trapped; display hostile animation toward Kevin; knockable unconscious when hit by traps
Patrol basement zones; hazard avoidance required; cannot be trapped like bandits; represent environmental danger rather than intelligent antagonism
Collects items; free movement; setting up traps; no combat pressure
Evades bandits; triggers prepared traps; defensive positioning; survival-focused
Navigates hazards; engages boss; moves toward safe room; climactic challenge engagement

notes: Home Alone (SNES) uses clear phase-based world state management: preparation (safe), threat activation (danger), environmental escalation (basement), and resolution (victory/defeat). Visual manifestation relies on sprite presence/absence (bandits appear = threat) and environmental transitions (mansion to basement = danger escalation). Faction identity is simple: player vs. criminals vs. supernatural hazards. No sophisticated faction signaling beyond sprite recognition. Regional differences (HUD labels: 'Power'/'Lives' vs. 'Life'/'Kevin') are UI cosmetic, not world-state manifestation. The 'haunted basement' represents deliberate tonal escalation beyond film narrative—environmental storytelling through location design rather than visual effects or color coding.
